evals.name: tool-agnostic-slide-too-busy
evals.prompt: Here's one of my slides — it has a paragraph of text, three charts, and a table. Is it too busy? How should I fix it?
evals.expected_behavior: Triggers slide-design-principles (not a format builder). Diagnoses density/one-signal violation, rewrites the title as a claim, splits the slide, and points to the specific rules applied — without emitting .tex or .pptx.

evals.name: what-goes-on-this-slide
evals.prompt: I have my results but I don't know what should actually go on the results slide. Help me decide what to show vs say.
evals.expected_behavior: Produces a slide-spec block (message, body, visual provenance, notes), separating on-slide evidence from speaker notes. Stays tool-agnostic; does not assume Beamer or PowerPoint.

evals.name: not-deck-generation
evals.prompt: Generate the full Beamer .tex for my 20-minute conference talk.
evals.expected_behavior: Defers to beamer-academic-deck for artifact generation rather than handling it here; slide-design-principles only supplies the spec schema and rules.
